channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot is adding AI agents for routine tasks
item.description: Microsoft will allow its customers to build autonomous artificial intelligence agents from next month, in its latest push to tap the booming technology amid growing investor scrutiny of its hefty AI investments.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Rejection therapy is all the rage on TikTok. Don’t bank on it working
item.description: Being rejected is never fun—or is it? Most of us spend our lives trying to wipe these embarrassing moments from memory, however, on TikTok a recent trend sees people actively seeking out opportunities to get shot down, then filming and uploading the awkward encounter for millions to see.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft Unveils Copilot Studio's AI Agent Capabilities - A New Era in Enterprise Automation
item.description: Microsoft's latest innovation enables businesses to build autonomous AI agents, marking a significant step forward in enterprise automation and digital transformation.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: How BSH boosted their knowledge power to deliver better customer service
item.description: The approximately 60,000 employees of BSH Home Appliances Group (BSH) would agree with a phrase invented more than 400 years ago by an Englishman, Sir Francis Bacon.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. Meta partnership, new tools will test that
item.description: “We play a really important role helping people ask for help and give help in the world,” GoFundMe CEO Tim Cadogan said.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korean Group Expands Operations: Companies Warned of Data Theft Risks
item.description: North Korea's Nickel Tapestry group expands operations, warns companies about data theft risks
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Estancamiento de la IA: la brecha entre la inversión en IA y su adopción
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) sigue dando forma a las estrategias en la nube, pero su implantación está siendo más lenta de lo previsto. Esto resulta frustrante para los proveedores de tecnología que han hecho grandes apuestas por la IA.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Recycling trucks with AI-powered cameras raise privacy concerns
item.description: The artificial intelligence can detect if there are any improperly recycled items and will log the information accordingly.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: This 10-second tweak will bring new life to any browser
item.description: When it comes to most home screens—on your phone, your computer, you name it—you’ve got endless-seeming options for customizing and taking control. The one canvas you can’t easily paint, curiously enough, is your browser’s New Tab page.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: Miss having a dedicated work phone? Here’s how to configure your iPhone to separate business from personal use
item.description: Smartphones have enabled us to attend to work from nearly anywhere. But their productivity power also frequently encroaches on our personal lives—making it too easy to reply to business emails or Slack messages when we should be focused on dinner with the fam.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Two Virginia congressional candidates debated an AI chatbot. It was shockingly rational
item.description: On Thursday night an AI chatbot participated in a political debate with candidates for a congressional seat, and, despite a few technical glitches, performed well. There was no robot voice, no off-topic tangents, no personal attacks, no odd “they’re eating the dogs” hallucinations.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: A federal judge has delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump family will get 75 percent of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Scientists show how sperm and egg come together like a key in a lock
item.description: Scientists have pinpointed how sperm and egg fuse together across the animal kingdom, from fish to people.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How you can help protect your aging parents from scammers
item.description: "They were raised to be a very trusting generation, a very rule following generation, so they make the assumption that everyone has their best interest at heart," Paula Daigle said.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Wielgus, NewsNation

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese cybersecurity association urges review of Intel products
item.description: The Cybersecurity Association of China (CSAC) has urged a security review of Intel products sold in the country, claiming the US semiconductor firm poses ongoing threats to China’s national security and interests.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: iOS 18: Maximize your privacy by turning off these iPhone settings now
item.description: Tech guru Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals some of the iPhone settings you may want to turn off to improve privacy after updating to iOS 18.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: AI isn’t really that smart yet, Apple researchers warn
item.description: While we wait for the Age Of Apple Intelligence, it may be worth considering a recent Apple research study that exposes critical weaknesses in existing artificial intelligence models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: WasmGC and the future of front-end Java development
item.description: JavaScript is the clear choice for front-end development today, but there is a longstanding push to be able to use Java on the front end. Among other things, Java brings a vast ecosystem of libraries, frameworks, and application code, which can be exposed via its APIs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Adobe starts roll-out of AI video tools, challenging OpenAI and Meta
item.description: Facing much larger rivals, Adobe has staked its future on building models trained on data that it has rights to use, ensuring the output can be legally used in commercial work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nvidia contributes Blackwell rack design to Open Compute Project
item.description: In the competitive world of enterprise IT vendors, it may be a surprise that some companies are freely sharing their designs, but that’s exactly what the 50-plus voting members and more than 300 community members and startups, including the major hyperscalers and chipmakers, of the Open Compute Proj
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World
